title: [UI Evolution · B2+B3] Wire density into table.tsx + add density toggle to AppHeader
description:
## Part B2: Wire density variables into table.tsx (cascades to all 11 Work Surfaces)

This is the single highest-impact change in the entire evolution. Replace 4 hardcoded values in `table.tsx` with CSS variable references. This cascades density to all 11 Work Surfaces automatically.

### File

`client/src/components/ui/table.tsx`

### Exact Changes

**TableHead:**

* `h-10` → `h-[var(--density-row-h)]`
* `px-2` → `px-[var(--density-cell-px)]`
* Add: `text-[length:var(--density-font-size)]`

**TableCell:**

* `p-2` → `px-[var(--density-cell-px)] py-[var(--density-cell-py)]`
* Add: `text-[length:var(--density-font-size)]`

Note: `text-[length:var(...)]` uses Tailwind arbitrary value with type hint because Tailwind cannot infer type from CSS variable.

### Reference

* [table-diff.md](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/reference-implementations/table-diff.md>) — Exact before/after diff
* [AGENT_PROMPT.md](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/AGENT_PROMPT.md>)

### Why It Works

The `:root` defaults match the current hardcoded values exactly. When `.density-compact` is absent, the UI is pixel-identical. When DensityContext adds the class, compact overrides activate everywhere.

---

## Part B3: Add density toggle button to AppHeader toolbar

### File

`client/src/components/layout/AppHeader.tsx`

### Implementation

* Import `useDensity` from DensityContext
* Add icon button (LayoutGrid / LayoutList from lucide-react) next to existing toolbar actions
* Button calls `toggleDensity()` on click
* Only visible when `ui-density-toggle` feature flag is enabled
* Tooltip shows current mode (Comfortable / Compact)

### Reference

* [AGENT_PROMPT.md](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/AGENT_PROMPT.md>)

---

## Acceptance Criteria (both B2 + B3)

- [ ] Comfortable mode is pixel-identical to current UI
- [ ] Compact mode shows visibly tighter rows (\~28px vs 40px)
- [ ] All 11 Work Surfaces respond to the density toggle
- [ ] Toggle button appears in AppHeader when flag is ON
- [ ] Toggle button hidden when flag is OFF
- [ ] Clicking toggles density across the entire app
- [ ] Visual feedback indicates current mode
- [ ] No TypeScript errors

## Density Variables

```css
:root {
  --density-row-h: 2.5rem;    /* 40px — matches current */
  --density-cell-px: 0.5rem;  /* 8px — matches current */
  --density-cell-py: 0.5rem;
  --density-font-size: 0.875rem;
  --density-sidebar-item-h: 2.25rem;
}
.density-compact {
  --density-row-h: 1.75rem;   /* 28px */
  --density-cell-px: 0.375rem;
  --density-cell-py: 0.25rem;
  --density-font-size: 0.8125rem;

}
```

Gated behind `ui-density-toggle` flag. When flag is OFF, DensityContext defaults to comfortable and the toggle UI is hidden.

title: [UI Evolution] Frontend Evolution — Brushed Gunmetal Redesign
description:
## Overview

Evolution of the TERP frontend from a modern app aesthetic to a professional power-tool UI ("brushed gunmetal"). This is a surgical, additive evolution — not a redesign or refactor.

**Branch:** `feat/ui-evolution`
**Effort:** 9–14 dev-days
**Scope:** 30 files modified, 6 files created, \~662 lines touched

## Architecture

| Part | What | Flagged? | Effort |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| A: Visual Migration | Token swap, font, radius, color cleanup | No | 2–3 days |
| B: Density System | CSS variable compact mode with toggle | Yes | 2–3 days |
| C: Power-User Features | Command palette v2, keyboard hints | Yes | 5–8 days |

## Handoff Package

All specs, design tokens, and reference implementations are in the repo:

* [AGENT_PROMPT.md](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/AGENT_PROMPT.md>) — Full execution instructions
* [CHANGE_MANIFEST.md](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/CHANGE_MANIFEST.md>) — File-by-file checklist
* [DESIGN_TOKENS.css](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/DESIGN_TOKENS.css>) — Drop-in token replacement
* [README.md](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/blob/main/docs/ui-evolution/README.md>) — Quick-start guide
* [Reference Implementations](<https://github.com/EvanTenenbaum/TERP/tree/main/docs/ui-evolution/reference-implementations>) — Working code for all new files

## Design Direction

Brushed gunmetal — machined precision, not app store gloss. IBM Plex fonts, OKLCH hue 250 (steel-blue gray), 4px max border radius, dark mode primary. See AGENT_PROMPT.md for full design system and visual references.

## Acceptance Criteria

* All 8 golden flows pass manual smoke test
* No TypeScript errors
* Parts B and C gated behind feature flags (disabled by default)
* Dark mode and light mode both work
* PR includes before/after screenshots

title: Wire LOT_ALLOCATION_POLICY into actual allocation flows
description:
Source: Adversarial QA review of Phase 5 (TER-225)
Severity: P2

Problem: LOT_ALLOCATION_POLICY constant and getBatchesFIFO function exist as scaffolding but are not consumed by any allocation flow. The order creation process still takes explicit batchIds without enforcing FIFO ordering. The constant is dead code.

Files:

* server/inventoryDb.ts (LOT_ALLOCATION_POLICY, getBatchesFIFO)
* server/ordersDb.ts (createOrder)

Acceptance criteria:

* LOT_ALLOCATION_POLICY is consumed by at least one allocation decision
* When auto-allocating batches for an order, FIFO ordering is enforced
* Manual batch selection still allowed as explicit override
* Test covers FIFO ordering behavior

title: Add transaction wrapper for productCatalogue.create duplicate check (TOCTOU)
description:
Source: Adversarial QA review of Phase 5 (TER-226)
Severity: P2

Problem: productCatalogue.create checks for duplicates via findDuplicateProduct then inserts via createProduct as two separate queries. Under concurrent requests, two callers can both pass the check and both insert, creating true duplicates. There is no unique constraint on products(nameCanonical, brandId) in the schema.

Files:

* server/routers/productCatalogue.ts
* server/productsDb.ts
* drizzle/schema.ts

Acceptance criteria:

* Either wrap check+insert in a transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE
* Or add a unique index on products(nameCanonical, brandId) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
* Race condition test added

title: Add test coverage for DirectIntakeWorkSurface
description:
Source: Adversarial QA review of Phase 4 (TER-217..224)
Severity: P2

Problem: DirectIntakeWorkSurface.tsx (1500+ lines) has zero test coverage. Multiple features were added in TER-217..224 without tests: multi-row acceleration, smart search, inline vendor create, subcategory field, notes traceability.

File: client/src/components/work-surface/DirectIntakeWorkSurface.tsx

Acceptance criteria:

* Test file created at **tests**/DirectIntakeWorkSurface.test.tsx
* Tests cover: initial 5 rows rendered, +5 Rows button adds rows, subcategory field exists, notes indicator visible
* 80%+ coverage target for new code paths

title: Enforce duplicate-name guardrails for products across batches
description: Interview evidence\\n- \[08:25.8 - 09:03.0\] should not allow exact same product name across batches\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Duplicate naming rule is enforced at validation + persistence layers\\n- Conflict resolution UX provided (reuse existing product vs create variant)\\n- Import/intake flows surface deterministic error messages

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[17:56.8 - 18:25.8\] client should auto-pull pricing rules\\n- \[19:08.8 - 19:13.8\] both should be set automatically by customer pricing rules\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Selecting client applies rule-based retail pricing by default\\n- Manual override still possible with clear source indicators\\n- Pricing rule application is deterministic and test-covered

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[10:19.0 - 10:28.0\] needs a save button\\n- \[12:43.8 - 13:09.8\] should be able to undo/edit\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- User can explicitly save in-progress edits\\n- Undo action reverts latest line-item and adjustment changes\\n- Edit history is consistent with autosave and finalization

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[17:37.8 - 17:50.8\] need same better filtering everywhere with browsing\\n\\nScope\\n- Orders/Sales create, Sales Sheets, Inventory, Products\\n- Shared filter primitives and consistent interactions\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Common filter behavior (apply, clear, persistence) across covered surfaces\\n- Shared component contract documented\\n- Cross-page filter regressions covered by interaction tests

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[05:24.9 - 05:52.9\], \[06:24.9 - 06:54.4\], \[12:13.8 - 12:26.8\]\\n\\nCode verification against deployed commit 7e5547bac498a75d7deb9cc7a8cbdca933d9b42b:\\n- `client/src/pages/OrderCreatorPage.tsx` uses a two-column layout (`lg:grid-cols-3`) with main browsing/line-item work on left (`lg:col-span-2`) and totals/preview on right (`1/3`)\\n- Preview has expand/collapse control via `<details><summary>Order Preview</summary>...`\\n- However, it is currently `details open`, so preview is expanded by default (does not yet satisfy "defaults collapsed")\\n\\nStatus rationale\\n- Kept as TODO because acceptance criteria are only partially met (layout is aligned; default collapsed behavior still pending).\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Main browse/filter area is primary focus\\n- Line items panel anchored on right and remains usable on common desktop widths\\n- Order preview defaults collapsed with explicit expand/collapse control

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[04:02.5 - 04:36.2\] click should select full value\\n- \[04:36.2 - 05:03.5\] stepper should increment by whole units, not points\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Numeric fields auto-select full value on focus/click\\n- Quantity steppers use configured whole-unit increments for relevant UOMs\\n- Decimal precision is explicit by field type

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[03:25.0 - 04:24.0\] quick category/subcategory filters and stock/price ranges\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Filter bar supports category, subcategory, stock min/max, price min/max\\n- Filters persist while editing line items\\n- Results count and clear-all behavior are deterministic

description: Interview evidence\\n- \[02:46.0 - 03:25.0\] inline editable amount and order limit\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Quantity and order limit editable inline in main browsing/sheet area\\n- Edits update totals immediately with validation feedback\\n- Keyboard-first operation supported (enter/tab/escape)

project.name: TERP - Immediate Phase: Broken Buttons & Interaction Reliability

title: Test auth fixtures: remove silent admin fallback for role-specific critical paths
description: Problem\\nRole-based and interaction tests can pass under admin fallback rather than intended role behavior.\\n\\nEvidence\\n`tests-e2e/fixtures/auth.ts`\\n- Non-admin login allows fallback to admin (lines 232-247)\\n- Fallback globally enabled unless env explicitly disables (`E2E_ALLOW_ADMIN_FALLBACK !== "false"`, line 250)\\n\\nRisk\\nFalse confidence: UI/control permissions and role-specific flows may appear healthy while actually untested for the target role.\\n\\nRequired fix\\n- Disable admin fallback by default for critical-path and RBAC suites\\n- Require explicit per-suite opt-in only where justified\\n- Surface role actually used in test output/artifacts\\n\\nAcceptance criteria\\n- Critical-path and RBAC suites fail when target role credentials/permissions are invalid\\n- Test reports include actual authenticated role per test\\n- No silent role substitution in core regression suite

project.name: TERP - Immediate Phase: Broken Buttons & Interaction Reliability

title: Restore prod-regression Playwright coverage (tag/project mismatch)
description:
Context: `pnpm test:e2e:prod-regression` currently exits with `Error: No tests found`, indicating coverage gap for production regression project.

Evidence

* Artifact: `/tmp/terp-live-validation/live-prod-regression-20260210-191659.log`

Scope

* Audit tagging strategy (`@prod-regression`) and project config in `playwright.config.ts`.
* Add/retag representative production-safe regression specs.
* Ensure CI/local command `test:e2e:prod-regression` consistently executes non-zero test set.

Acceptance criteria

* Command runs at least one regression test in production-safe mode.
* Regression suite documented and included in release validation checklist.
* Fail-fast guard added if project resolves to zero tests unexpectedly.

title: Add combobox-aware select action in oracle executor
description:
Context: `CRM.Clients.Create` failed because executor uses `selectOption` on a non-`<select>` combobox (`role=combobox` button in Radix/shadcn UI).

Evidence

* Error: `locator.selectOption: Error: Element is not a <select> element`
* Artifact: `/tmp/terp-live-validation/live-oracle-tier1-20260210-190449.log`

Scope

* Update `tests-e2e/oracles/executor.ts` select action to detect control type.
* For native `<select>`, keep `selectOption`.
* For button-based combobox/listbox, perform click-open + option-click strategy with robust fallback matching.
* Add regression tests for both control types.

Acceptance criteria

* Oracle `select` step succeeds on both native select and combobox implementations.
* `CRM.Clients.Create` no longer fails with non-select element errors.
* Executor behavior documented for future oracle authors.

title: Harden oracle preconditions for empty-state live data (ID resolution)
description:
Context: Tier1 oracles repeatedly hit `CANNOT_RESOLVE_ID` when expected entities are absent in live datasets (orders/confirm/fulfillment/update status flows).

Evidence

* Same run artifact: `/tmp/terp-live-validation/live-oracle-tier1-20260210-190449.log`
* Blocked flows include order-dependent scenarios where row IDs are not present.

Scope

* Improve preconditions and seed-profile behavior for live-mode oracle runs.
* Implement explicit empty-state handling strategy:
  * create prerequisite entity when safe, or
  * mark deterministic BLOCKED with structured reason and skip downstream dependent steps.
* Ensure dependency chains between draft/order/invoice flows are explicit.

Acceptance criteria

* Order-dependent Tier1 flows no longer fail due to unresolved IDs.
* Flows are either executable or deterministically BLOCKED with prerequisite rationale.
* Blocked count attributable only to true business precondition constraints.

title: Reduce false negatives in oracle validation signals (error/loading/content heuristics)
description:
Background:
Validation heuristics (`error/loading/content`) can produce false negatives despite successful UI states, especially on dashboard/list pages.

Scope:

1. Re-tune signal checks to focus on visible, semantically meaningful indicators.
2. Fix URL assertion handling for full URLs vs path-only regex expectations.
3. Add per-domain overrides for acceptable loading artifacts and content thresholds.
4. Add unit tests for validation classifier edge cases.

Done when:

* Validation failures correlate with actual user-visible failures.
* Known false negatives are covered by tests.

title: Unify QA credential source-of-truth with production test users
description:
Background:
Production QA accounts have drifted from hardcoded assumptions in tests/docs. During live validation, only a subset of expected role accounts authenticated with shared password; others were renamed or had different credential hashes.

Scope:

1. Define canonical QA credential registry (env-driven first, no hardcoded defaults in runtime oracles).
2. Update docs/runbooks to reflect production-compatible account names.
3. Add CI guard/check that verifies all required role credentials can authenticate in target environment.
4. Remove stale role aliases from test defaults unless explicitly configured.

Done when:

* Runtime suite has no auth failures due stale account assumptions.
* Credential contract is centrally documented and validated.

title: Implement seed/precondition hydration for live oracle runs
description:
Background:
Oracle DSL uses `{{seed:...}}` and precondition references, but executor currently cannot resolve many entity IDs in live environments, producing blocked/failed flows (e.g. no matching orders/invoices for row-target selectors).

Scope:

1. Add live precondition resolver that can map `seed:` entity refs to real records (via deterministic lookup strategy).
2. Distinguish clearly between:

* BLOCKED (missing required live data)
* FAIL (actual app regression)

3. Add optional setup step for creating minimal ephemeral test entities when safe.
4. Emit structured blocked reasons in `oracle-results.json`.

Done when:

* Seed-based selectors no longer degrade to empty tokens.
* Flows requiring live fixture data are either executable or explicitly BLOCKED with machine-readable reason.

title: Stabilize Oracle selectors against live UI contracts (CRM/Inventory/Orders)
description:
Background:
Live runtime-oracle tier1 run on Feb 10, 2026 ([https://terp-app-b9s35.ondigitalocean.app](<https://terp-app-b9s35.ondigitalocean.app>)) still has broad failures after auth/module fixes due selector contract drift.

Evidence:

* Many flows fail on missing selectors such as `[data-testid='client-row']`, `[data-testid='invoice-detail']`, `[data-testid='business-type-select']`.
* Current UI shows real controls but with different selectors/structure (e.g. client grid + Add Client dialog).

Scope:

1. Build a canonical selector contract per module (CRM, Inventory, Orders, Accounting).
2. Update tier1 oracle YAML selectors to match current UI.
3. Add compatibility aliases in executor only where justified (avoid overbroad fallbacks).
4. Add regression check that validates selector existence for each oracle before runtime execution.

Done when:

* Tier1 runtime run no longer fails due selector-not-found errors on these modules.
* Selector contract report generated and checked in.

title: [Systemic] Add automatic E2E failure clustering + ticket sync pipeline
description:
Problem
Manual triage is slow; recurring failures are re-litigated run to run.

Deliverables

* Auto-cluster failures by signature/file/selector.
* Persist trend history across runs.
* Sync new clusters to Linear (create/update) with run links and artifacts.

Acceptance criteria

* Each run generates a clustered summary automatically.
* Existing open tickets are updated instead of duplicated where possible.
* Mean time to classify failures drops significantly.

title: [Systemic] Reconcile RBAC test contracts with live role assignments
description:
Problem
RBAC tests currently include assumptions that diverge from live assignments and permissions.

Deliverables

* Publish authoritative QA role-to-permission matrix from production.
* Update tests to use role-assigned accounts by default.
* Add guardrails for roleless legacy accounts.

Acceptance criteria

* RBAC failures are true permission regressions, not account mismatch.
* All RBAC specs reference canonical role fixtures.
* Roleless legacy users are excluded or explicitly handled.

title: [Systemic] Make feature-flag sensitive tests environment-aware
description:
Problem
Live Catalog / VIP / feature-management tests fail when flags/config are not enabled for test actors.

Deliverables

* Detect feature state before running feature-dependent tests.
* Route disabled-state checks to separate expectations.
* Add explicit setup instructions for environments that should run full feature scenarios.

Acceptance criteria

* Flag-dependent tests no longer fail due to disabled features.
* Reports clearly distinguish disabled-feature skips from regressions.
* Feature suite can be toggled on/off cleanly via env contract.

title: [Systemic] Align auth/session test expectations with production behavior
description:
Problem
Auth tests expect redirect-to-login behavior that conflicts with production runtime settings (e.g., demo/auto-auth flows).

Deliverables

* Decide and document canonical production auth behavior.
* Split tests for strict-auth mode vs demo-auth mode.
* Ensure logout/protected-route assertions match real environment contract.

Acceptance criteria

* Auth-related expectation mismatches are eliminated.
* Environment mode is explicit in test output.
* Protected-route tests are deterministic for each mode.

title: [Systemic] Implement production-latency aware timeout/wait policy
description:
Problem
A large block of failures are timeouts or late-loading UI state under real production conditions.

Deliverables

* Define per-action timeout standards for prod runs.
* Replace fixed sleeps with condition-based waits.
* Add retry wrappers for known eventually-consistent screens.
* Capture timing diagnostics on timeout for faster triage.

Acceptance criteria

* Timeout failures reduced by >=60%.
* Median run stability improves across 3 consecutive runs.
* Timeout diagnostics are attached automatically to failed tests.

title: [Systemic] Standardize resilient selector strategy across E2E specs
description:
Problem
Frequent failures from visibility/selector issues indicate brittle selector usage and strict-mode collisions.

Deliverables

* Prefer stable `data-testid` selectors over text/CSS fallbacks.
* Add lint/checks to reject ambiguous selectors in new tests.
* Introduce shared selector helpers and migration guide.
* Fix known selector syntax errors (`Unexpected token` parse failures).

Acceptance criteria

* Zero selector syntax parse failures.
* Strict-mode selector collisions reduced to near zero.
* New tests pass selector lint checks.

title: [Systemic] Add deterministic precondition guards for live production runs
description:
Problem
Many tests fail because required entities/states are absent in production at run time.

Why this matters
Tests should skip with explicit reason when prerequisites are missing, instead of hard-failing.

Deliverables

* Add reusable precondition helpers (record exists, feature enabled, role assigned, page reachable).
* Convert brittle tests to guard + skip semantics where behavior is optional.
* Emit standardized skip reasons in report.

Acceptance criteria

* Precondition-related failures are reduced by >=70%.
* Skips are intentional and categorized in report.
* No hard failure due solely to missing seed-only fixtures.

title: [Systemic] Split production-safe E2E suite from local/mock-dependent suite
description:
Problem
Current `chromium` run mixes production-targeted tests with tests that assume local/mocked behavior, causing avoidable failures and noisy signal.

Why this matters
Without strict suite boundaries, results are difficult to trust and remediation becomes whack-a-mole.

Deliverables

* Define explicit suite categories: `prod-smoke`, `prod-regression`, `local-contract`, `local-ui`.
* Ensure production runs only include tests compatible with live data + live auth.
* Move or tag incompatible specs out of production pipeline.
* Document run matrix and ownership.

Acceptance criteria

* Running production command produces only production-safe tests.
* No local/mock-dependent test appears in production run list.
* CI pipeline has separate jobs for production-safe vs local suites.

title: [P1] Media-08: Production seed needs a source (placeholder upload + attach to batches with 0 images)
description:
## Problem (plain English)

The new safe seeding script is correct/safe, but in production there are *no* existing image URLs to seed from:

* `product_images` has 0 rows
* `productMedia` has 0 rows
* `batches.metadata` is NULL (so no `metadata.mediaFiles`)

So a "seed from existing URLs" approach inserts nothing.

## Goal

Make production test data visually complete by attaching at least 1 image to each batch that currently has zero images.

## Requirements

* Must be safe: no deletes, idempotent, dry-run by default
* Must work even when production has 0 image URLs today
* Prefer storing image(s) in TERP’s normal media storage (not random external URLs)
* Mark seeded rows so we can audit/remove later if desired (caption prefix or uploadedBy)

## Approach Options

### Option A (recommended): upload 1-3 placeholder images once, then attach URLs to missing batches

1. Upload placeholder image(s) to the same storage used by `inventory.uploadMedia`
2. Get resulting URL(s)
3. Insert `product_images` rows for batches with zero images (set first as primary)

### Option B: use a stable external placeholder URL

Faster but worse long-term (external dependency + licensing risk).

## Acceptance

* A batch with no images gets exactly 1 primary shown image after seed
* Re-running seed does not duplicate
* Batch edit can hide/unhide, choose primary, reorder
* VIP/catalog surfaces show that primary image

parent.title: [P1] Media: Batch Image Lifecycle + Safe Seeding (Intake → Photography → Surfacing → VIP/Catalog)
